item.title: Meta to Discontinue Key Metaverse Product For VR Headsets
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. said that users of its Quest headsets will lose access to Horizon Worlds, a virtual destination where cartoon versions of people can meet up and play games, marking the latest pullback from a strategy once central to Mark Zuckerberg’s vision of a so-called metaverse.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Says Company Is Firing Up H200 Production for China
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said the company is firing up manufacturing of H200 AI accelerators for customers in China, a sign of progress in the chipmaker’s effort to reenter the vital market.

item.title: Barclays Says Soaring BDC Risk Premiums Are ‘Justified’
item.description: Investors are demanding higher risk premiums to own the debt of business development companies, reflecting their anxiety around private credit exposure, according to Barclays Plc.

item.title: AI Drone Software Stock Jumps 700% in Best IPO Since Newsmax
item.description: Swarmer Inc. shares skyrocketed 700% on Tuesday, putting the artificial intelligence drone software maker on pace for the best trading debut by a US stock since Newsmax Inc.’s blockbuster entry nearly a year ago.

item.title: Canadian Dollar’s Resilience at Risk as Central Bankers Meet
item.description: One of the most resilient currencies of early March — the Canadian dollar — is at risk of losing its edge as the nation’s central bankers gather this week to assess its weakened economy.

item.title: Barclays’ Altmann Sees Strongest Buy Signal for Stocks in a Year
item.description: US stocks are flashing their strongest buy signal in almost a year, according to Barclays’ Alex Altmann, who joins a growing chorus on Wall Street saying the worst of the recent rout may be over.

item.title: Trump Again Vows to Block Wind Turbines During His Presidency
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he wanted to have no wind turbines built during his presidency, reiterating his distaste for the renewable energy source after his administration has made multiple moves to thwart its development.

item.title: JPMorgan Halts Qualtrics $5.3 Billion Debt Deal on Software Pain
item.description: A group of banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. halted a $5.3 billion debt deal for software firm Qualtrics International Inc. after failing to win over investors amid deepening anxiety surrounding artificial intelligence disruption.

item.title: Spain Weighs Buying More Algerian Gas as War Tightens Supplies
item.description: Spain is considering buying more natural gas via pipeline from Algeria to help boost supplies as the war in the Middle East is causing a surge in prices.

item.title: Alcoa Draws New Aluminum Orders as Gulf States Curb Production
item.description: Alcoa Corp., the largest US aluminum producer, says it’s fielding interest from buyers seeking alternative supply following the curtailment of production in the Middle East.

item.title: Redwood Capital Among Creditors to Get Control of Bankrupt REIT
item.description: Office Properties Income Trust, the bankrupt owner of more than 120 office buildings, is nearing a deal to settle a months-long restructuring case by handing ownership to creditors in exchange for cutting its debt load by $700 million to about $1.7 billion.

item.title: EV Startup Olinia Powers Up in Mexico Thanks to State Support
item.description: Mexico’s government-backed electric vehicle startup Olinia will unveil its first two prototypes in June as it seeks $200 million in private capital to jumpstart manufacturing, all part of an ambitious plan to transform how people move around cities.

item.title: Signs of Indigestion in the Bond Market
item.description: A big change from a few months ago

item.title: Xanadu Moves Closer to Public Listing as Quantum Race Heats Up
item.description: Startup Xanadu Quantum Technologies Inc. is preparing a public listing with the promise that it’s going to build one of the first quantum-powered data centers by 2030. If successful, the deal would break a long drought.

item.title: Arizona Charges Kalshi With Illegal Gambling Operation
item.description: Arizona said it filed criminal charges against Kalshi for operating an illegal gambling business, in a significant escalation in the legal battles between the prediction market exchange and about a dozen states.

item.title: Nvidia Sees Sales Goal Topping $1 Trillion With New Markets
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said the $1 trillion projection he issued a day ago for AI chip sales doesn’t capture other product offerings and signaled that total revenue will surpass that level as the company pushes into new markets.

item.title: Perplexity AI Shopping Bots Can Stay on Amazon for Now, Court Rules
item.description: Perplexity AI Inc. won a reprieve from a court order that would have banned its Comet shopping bots from Amazon.com Inc. this week.

item.title: Sri Lanka Growth Slows on Cyclone; Outlook Clouded by Conflict
item.description: Sri Lanka’s economic growth slowed in the fourth quarter due to the impact of Cyclone Ditwah, while the near-term outlook remains clouded by the Iran war.

item.title: Tencent Seizes Momentum in China Agentic AI Race Against Alibaba
item.description: In China’s hyper-competitive AI arena, Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. has outpaced Tencent Holdings Ltd. with the sheer speed of rollouts and user growth. But the latter firm is now seizing the initiative as agentic AI fever grips the country.

item.title: Pentagon Moving to Replace Anthropic Amid AI Feud, Official Says
item.description: The Pentagon is working to develop alternatives to Anthropic PBC’s artificial intelligence tools, according to a senior US defense official, following a Trump administration decision to declare the company a supply-chain risk in a feud over safeguards governing military use of the technology.

item.title: 道明银行称，罗杰斯或将出售其持有的这家市值180亿美元体育巨头的股份以削减债务
item.description: Rogers Communications Inc. could sell off almost one-third of a C$25 billion ($18 billion) Canadian sports empire to pay down debt this year, according to analysts at TD Securities.

item.title: Asian Refiners Scour the World for Oil With Hormuz Flows Halted
item.description: Asia’s oil refiners are ramping up crude purchases from outside the Middle East, the latest sign of a scramble to secure supplies as shipping through the vital Strait of Hormuz remains all-but halted.

item.title: Bank of Canada Likely to Stay on Hold as Oil Scrambles the Outlook
item.description: The Bank of Canada is likely to hold interest rates steady as policymakers weigh the inflation risk of higher oil prices against a string of weak economic numbers.

item.title: IBM CEO Sees More AI Deals in ‘Friendlier’ Regulatory Climate
item.description: International Business Machines Corp. Chief Executive Officer Arvind Krishna expects the company to pursue more deals in AI, helped by less regulatory pushback.

item.title: Greece Pushes LNG Hub Role in Europe’s Post-Russian Gas Era
item.description: Greece is positioning itself as a key gas hub for central and southeastern Europe as the continent moves toward a full phaseout of Russian energy, Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis said.

item.title: 随着油价波动打乱利率预期，巴西加大了债券市场干预力度
item.description: Brazil’s Treasury intervened in the local bond market for a second straight day, announcing fresh auctions to both buy back and sell government debt to support liquidity and stabilize trading as the surge in crude prices ripples through global markets.

item.title: Microsoft Combines Workplace, Personal Copilot Teams in AI Push
item.description: Microsoft Corp. is uniting the teams that build versions of its Copilot for individuals and corporations, streamlining product development as the company works to get its AI assistant into the hands of more customers.

item.title: UK Funds Snap Up Gilts in Bet That Markets Have BOE All Wrong
item.description: Two of Britain’s biggest asset managers are buying UK government bonds, convinced the market has misjudged how the Bank of England will respond to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Reeves Floats Plan to Give UK Regions Say Over Income Tax Funds
item.description: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said she is drawing up plans to give regional leaders in the UK control of some national tax revenues, as part of her push to raise economic growth across Britain.

item.title: North Sea Oil Tycoon Faces Asset Freeze Over Iran Plant Dealings
item.description: Francesco Mazzagatti, an energy trader who has become a key figure in the North Sea industry, is facing a worldwide asset freeze over allegations he siphoned off funds from former business partners in Iran.
